overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, with a total following of 3580. His content focuses on research, job opportunities, and discussions related to social media analysis, disinformation, and computational social science. LinkedIn has the largest following, while Bluesky shows a good balance of likes and reposts. Engagement varies across platforms, with LinkedIn posts generally receiving the most likes and comments.

contentThemes.description: Posts related to research findings, publications, and methodologies in the field of social media analysis, disinformation, and information operations.
Content focused on computational methods, algorithms, and data science techniques applied to social science research.
Posts announcing job openings, research grants, and PhD positions within Fabio's research team.
Content about upcoming conferences, presentations, and speaking engagements related to the field.

recommendations: Tailor content to each platform's audience. For example, more professional updates and job postings on LinkedIn.
Use platform-specific hashtags to increase visibility and reach a wider audience.
Engage with followers by responding to comments and questions to build a stronger community.
Experiment with different content formats (e.g., images, videos) to see what resonates best with each platform's audience.
Analyze post performance regularly to identify trends and optimize future content strategy.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky shows good engagement in terms of both likes and reposts. Focus on sharing research papers and opportunities for PhD students.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has a smaller audience but is engaged with coordinated link-sharing research. Continue to post updates on CooRnet and similar projects.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn boasts the largest follower base. Leverage this platform for professional announcements, grant news, and recruitment efforts.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
